fields.title: Default agent profile
fields.description: Subdirectory of /agents folder to be used by default agent no. 0. Subordinate agents can be spawned with other profiles, that is on their superior agent to decide. This setting affects the behaviour of the top level agent you communicate with.

fields.title: Knowledge subdirectory
fields.description: Subdirectory of /knowledge folder to use for agent knowledge import. 'default' subfolder is always imported and contains framework knowledge.

sections.title: Chat Model
sections.description: Selection and settings for main chat model used by Agent Zero

fields.title: Chat model provider
fields.description: Select provider for main chat model used by Agent Zero

fields.title: Chat model name
fields.description: Exact name of model from selected provider

fields.title: Chat model API base URL
fields.description: API base URL for main chat model. Leave empty for default. Only relevant for Azure, local and custom (other) providers.

fields.title: Chat model context length
fields.description: Maximum number of tokens in the context window for LLM. System prompt, chat history, RAG and response all count towards this limit.

fields.title: Context window space for chat history
fields.description: Portion of context window dedicated to chat history visible to the agent. Chat history will automatically be optimized to fit. Smaller size will result in shorter and more summarized history. The remaining space will be used for system prompt, RAG and response.

fields.title: Supports Vision
fields.description: Models capable of Vision can for example natively see the content of image attachments.

fields.title: Requests per minute limit
fields.description: Limits the number of requests per minute to the chat model. Waits if the limit is exceeded. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.

fields.title: Input tokens per minute limit
fields.description: Limits the number of input tokens per minute to the chat model. Waits if the limit is exceeded. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.

fields.title: Output tokens per minute limit
fields.description: Limits the number of output tokens per minute to the chat model. Waits if the limit is exceeded. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.

fields.title: Chat model additional parameters
fields.description: Any other parameters supported by LiteLLM. Format is KEY=VALUE on individual lines, like .env file. Value can also contain JSON objects - when unquoted, it is treated as object, number etc., when quoted, it is treated as string.

sections.title: Utility model
sections.description: Smaller, cheaper, faster model for handling utility tasks like organizing memory, preparing prompts, summarizing.

fields.title: Utility model provider
fields.description: Select provider for utility model used by the framework

fields.title: Utility model name
fields.description: Exact name of model from selected provider

fields.title: Utility model API base URL
fields.description: API base URL for utility model. Leave empty for default. Only relevant for Azure, local and custom (other) providers.

fields.title: Requests per minute limit
fields.description: Limits the number of requests per minute to the utility model. Waits if the limit is exceeded. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.

fields.title: Input tokens per minute limit
fields.description: Limits the number of input tokens per minute to the utility model. Waits if the limit is exceeded. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.

fields.title: Output tokens per minute limit
fields.description: Limits the number of output tokens per minute to the utility model. Waits if the limit is exceeded. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.

fields.title: Utility model additional parameters
fields.description: Any other parameters supported by LiteLLM. Format is KEY=VALUE on individual lines, like .env file. Value can also contain JSON objects - when unquoted, it is treated as object, number etc., when quoted, it is treated as string.

sections.title: Web Browser Model
sections.description: Settings for the web browser model. Agent Zero uses browser-use agentic framework to handle web interactions.

fields.title: Web Browser model provider
fields.description: Select provider for web browser model used by browser-use framework

fields.title: Web Browser model name
fields.description: Exact name of model from selected provider

fields.title: Web Browser model API base URL
fields.description: API base URL for web browser model. Leave empty for default. Only relevant for Azure, local and custom (other) providers.

fields.title: Use Vision
fields.description: Models capable of Vision can use it to analyze web pages from screenshots. Increases quality but also token usage.

fields.title: Web Browser model rate limit requests
fields.description: Rate limit requests for web browser model.

fields.title: Web Browser model rate limit input
fields.description: Rate limit input for web browser model.

fields.title: Web Browser model rate limit output
fields.description: Rate limit output for web browser model.

fields.title: Web Browser model additional parameters
fields.description: Any other parameters supported by LiteLLM. Format is KEY=VALUE on individual lines, like .env file. Value can also contain JSON objects - when unquoted, it is treated as object, number etc., when quoted, it is treated as string.

fields.title: HTTP Headers
fields.description: HTTP headers to include with all browser requests. Format is KEY=VALUE on individual lines, like .env file. Value can also contain JSON objects - when unquoted, it is treated as object, number etc., when quoted, it is treated as string. Example: Authorization=Bearer token123

sections.title: Embedding Model
sections.description:
Settings for the embedding model used by Agent Zero.

⚠️ No need to change

The default HuggingFace model sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 is preloaded and runs locally within the docker container and there's no need to change it unless you have a specific requirements for embedding.

fields.title: Embedding model provider
fields.description: Select provider for embedding model used by the framework

fields.title: Embedding model name
fields.description: Exact name of model from selected provider

fields.title: Embedding model API base URL
fields.description: API base URL for embedding model. Leave empty for default. Only relevant for Azure, local and custom (other) providers.

fields.title: Requests per minute limit
fields.description: Limits the number of requests per minute to the embedding model. Waits if the limit is exceeded. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.

fields.title: Input tokens per minute limit
fields.description: Limits the number of input tokens per minute to the embedding model. Waits if the limit is exceeded. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.

fields.title: Embedding model additional parameters
fields.description: Any other parameters supported by LiteLLM. Format is KEY=VALUE on individual lines, like .env file. Value can also contain JSON objects - when unquoted, it is treated as object, number etc., when quoted, it is treated as string.

sections.title: Memory
sections.description: Configuration of A0's memory system. A0 memorizes and recalls memories automatically to help it's context awareness.

fields.title: Memory Subdirectory
fields.description: Subdirectory of /memory folder to use for agent memory storage. Used to separate memory storage between different instances.

fields.title: Memory Dashboard
fields.description: View and explore all stored memories in a table format with filtering and search capabilities.

fields.title: Memory auto-recall enabled
fields.description: Agent Zero will automatically recall memories based on convesation context.

fields.title: Memory auto-recall delayed
fields.description: The agent will not wait for auto memory recall. Memories will be delivered one message later. This speeds up agent's response time but may result in less relevant first step.

fields.title: Auto-recall AI query preparation
fields.description: Enables vector DB query preparation from conversation context by utility LLM for auto-recall. Improves search quality, adds 1 utility LLM call per auto-recall.

fields.title: Auto-recall AI post-filtering
fields.description: Enables memory relevance filtering by utility LLM for auto-recall. Improves search quality, adds 1 utility LLM call per auto-recall.

fields.title: Memory auto-recall interval
fields.description: Memories are recalled after every user or superior agent message. During agent's monologue, memories are recalled every X turns based on this parameter.

fields.title: Memory auto-recall history length
fields.description: The length of conversation history passed to memory recall LLM for context (in characters).

fields.title: Memory auto-recall similarity threshold
fields.description: The threshold for similarity search in memory recall (0 = no similarity, 1 = exact match).

fields.title: Memory auto-recall max memories to search
fields.description: The maximum number of memories returned by vector DB for further processing.

fields.title: Memory auto-recall max memories to use
fields.description: The maximum number of memories to inject into A0's context window.

fields.title: Memory auto-recall max solutions to search
fields.description: The maximum number of solutions returned by vector DB for further processing.

fields.title: Memory auto-recall max solutions to use
fields.description: The maximum number of solutions to inject into A0's context window.

fields.title: Auto-memorize enabled
fields.description: A0 will automatically memorize facts and solutions from conversation history.

fields.title: Auto-memorize AI consolidation
fields.description: A0 will automatically consolidate similar memories using utility LLM. Improves memory quality over time, adds 2 utility LLM calls per memory.

fields.title: Auto-memorize replacement threshold
fields.description: Only applies when AI consolidation is disabled. Replaces previous similar memories with new ones based on this threshold. 0 = replace even if not similar at all, 1 = replace only if exact match.

sections.title: Speech
sections.description: Voice transcription and speech synthesis settings.

fields.title: Microphone device
fields.description: Select the microphone device to use for speech-to-text.

fields.title: Speech-to-text model size
fields.description: Select the speech-to-text model size

fields.title: Speech-to-text language code
fields.description: Language code (e.g. en, fr, it)

fields.title: Microphone silence threshold
fields.description: Silence detection threshold. Lower values are more sensitive to noise.

fields.title: Microphone silence duration (ms)
fields.description: Duration of silence before the system considers speaking to have ended.

fields.title: Microphone waiting timeout (ms)
fields.description: Duration of silence before the system closes the microphone.

fields.title: Enable Kokoro TTS
fields.description: Enable higher quality server-side AI (Kokoro) instead of browser-based text-to-speech.

sections.title: API Keys
sections.description:
API keys for model providers and services used by Agent Zero. You can set multiple API keys separated by a comma (,). They will be used in round-robin fashion.
For more information abou Agent Zero Venice provider, see Agent Zero Venice.

sections.title: Cloudflare Workers AI
sections.description: Manage Cloudflare Workers AI credentials, health, rotation, and testing. The default recommended model is @cf/openai/gpt-oss-120b.

sections.title: LiteLLM Global Settings
sections.description: Configure global parameters passed to LiteLLM for all providers.

fields.title: LiteLLM global parameters
fields.description: Global LiteLLM params (e.g. timeout, stream_timeout) in .env format: one KEY=VALUE per line. Example: stream_timeout=30. Applied to all LiteLLM calls unless overridden. See LiteLLM and timeouts.

sections.title: Secrets Management
sections.description: Manage secrets and credentials that agents can use without exposing values to LLMs, chat history or logs. Placeholders are automatically replaced with values just before tool calls. If bare passwords occur in tool results, they are masked back to placeholders.

fields.title: Variables Store
fields.description:
Store non-sensitive variables in .env format e.g. EMAIL_IMAP_SERVER="imap.gmail.com", one item per line. You can use comments starting with # to add descriptions for the agent. See example.
These variables are visible to LLMs and in chat history, they are not being masked.

fields.title: Secrets Store
fields.description:
Store secrets and credentials in .env format e.g. EMAIL_PASSWORD="s3cret-p4$$w0rd", one item per line. You can use comments starting with # to add descriptions for the agent. See example.
These variables are not visile to LLMs and in chat history, they are being masked. ⚠️ only values with length >= 4 are being masked to prevent false positives.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.description: Settings for authentication to use Agent Zero Web UI.

fields.title: UI Login
fields.description: Set user name for web UI

fields.title: UI Password
fields.description: Set user password for web UI

sections.title: External MCP Servers
sections.description: Agent Zero can use external MCP servers, local or remote as tools.

fields.title: MCP Servers Configuration
fields.description: External MCP servers can be configured here.

fields.title: MCP Servers
fields.description: (JSON list of) >> RemoteServer <<: [name, url, headers, timeout (opt), sse_read_timeout (opt), disabled (opt)] / >> Local Server <<: [name, command, args, env, encoding (opt), encoding_error_handler (opt), disabled (opt)]

fields.title: MCP Client Init Timeout
fields.description: Timeout for MCP client initialization (in seconds). Higher values might be required for complex MCPs, but might also slowdown system startup.

fields.title: MCP Client Tool Timeout
fields.description: Timeout for MCP client tool execution. Higher values might be required for complex tools, but might also result in long responses with failing tools.

sections.title: A0 MCP Server
sections.description: Agent Zero can be exposed as an SSE MCP server. See connection example.

fields.title: Enable A0 MCP Server
fields.description: Expose Agent Zero as an SSE/HTTP MCP server. This will make this A0 instance available to MCP clients.

fields.title: MCP Server Token
fields.description: Token for MCP server authentication.

sections.title: A0 A2A Server
sections.description: Agent Zero can be exposed as an A2A server. See connection example.

fields.title: Enable A2A server
fields.description: Expose Agent Zero as A2A server. This allows other agents to connect to A0 via A2A protocol.

sections.title: External API
sections.description: Agent Zero provides external API endpoints for integration with other applications. These endpoints use API key authentication and support text messages and file attachments.

fields.title: API Examples
fields.description: View examples for using Agent Zero's external API endpoints with API key authentication.

sections.title: Backup & Restore
sections.description: Backup and restore Agent Zero data and configurations using glob pattern-based file selection.

fields.title: Create Backup
fields.description: Create a backup archive of selected files and configurations using customizable patterns.

fields.title: Restore from Backup
fields.description: Restore files and configurations from a backup archive with pattern-based selection.

sections.title: Development
sections.description: Parameters for A0 framework development. RFCs (remote function calls) are used to call functions on another A0 instance. You can develop and debug A0 natively on your local system while redirecting some functions to A0 instance in docker. This is crucial for development as A0 needs to run in standardized environment to support all features.

fields.title: Shell Interface
fields.description: Terminal interface used for Code Execution Tool. Local Python TTY works locally in both dockerized and development environments. SSH always connects to dockerized environment (automatically at localhost or RFC host address).

fields.title: RFC Destination URL
fields.description: URL of dockerized A0 instance for remote function calls. Do not specify port here.

fields.title: RFC Password
fields.description: Password for remote function calls. Passwords must match on both instances. RFCs can not be used with empty password.

fields.title: RFC HTTP port
fields.description: HTTP port for dockerized instance of A0.

fields.title: RFC SSH port
fields.description: SSH port for dockerized instance of A0.
